name: Power-Law Flat Adaptive Pi Radial Operator
description: In the flat-adaptive pi framework with power-law scaling lambda(r) proportional to r^beta, the radial operator reduces to the Euclidean radial Laplacian in an effective dimension of beta+2. This operator governs harmonic and Poisson equations in the flat-adaptive context.
assumptions: Flat-adaptive field scales as a power law lambda(r) proportional to r^beta.
beta > -2 to ensure finite area near r=0.
Radial functions considered only, no angular dependence.
evidence: Recovers the ordinary 2D radial Laplacian when beta=0.
Equivalent to the Euclidean radial Laplacian in effective dimension beta+2.
Closed-form harmonic and Poisson solutions exist via Bessel functions.
